content.full_text:
CRAIG S.J. JOHNS
1999 Harrison Street
Oakland, CA 94612
(510) 763-2000


September 18, 1992


Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "I" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95812


Re:     Request for Formal Advice Regarding Potential
        Conflict of Interests


Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am writing to request formal advice pursuant to California Government Code
Section 83114(b) and 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18328(b) regarding my
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (Cal. Gov't.
Code § 81000 et. seq. and 2 Cal. Code Regs. § 18700 et. seq.). I am a Member of the
California Regional Water Quality Control Board, San Francisco Bay Region ("Regional
Board"). This letter contains all facts material to my inquiry.

The circumstances underlying this request are as follows. I am an Associate with
the Oakland law firm of Crosby, Heafey, Roach & May Professional Corporation
("Crosby"). I am a salaried employee of Crosby and enjoy no financial or other ownership
interest in the firm. The firm represents a number of clients in a variety of legal
matters. Some of these clients occasionally have, or are interested in, matters which are
before the Regional Board and require some action on the part of the Regional Board,
including, for example, permit issuance, development and adoption of the Regional Water
Quality Basin Plan, or enforcement of applicable water quality statutes and regulations.

On the agenda before the Regional Board for the month of October 1992 is a
Tentative Order that would amend certain Waste Discharge Requirements (the "Tentative
Order") for Shell Oil Company ("Shell"), Martinez Manufacturing Complex. I have
enclosed, for your information, a draft copy of the Tentative Order that will be presented
to the Regional Board in October.

Crosby represents Shell in matters regarding tort liability issues related to Shell's
facility in Martinez. Crosby does not represent Shell in any matters related to the

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
September 18, 1992
Page 2


Tentative Order before the Regional Board in October, 1992. I receive no reportable
income or gifts from Shell, am involved in no contractual matters with Shell, and I own
no stock or other securities in Shell.

Under these circumstances, is a conflict of interest deemed to exist which would
require me to abstain from participating in, voting on, or attempting to influence a
decision concerning the Shell matter pending before the Regional Board? I am
particularly interested in your opinion regarding the question presented in light of In re
Pirner, 1 FPPC Ops. 198 (1975), and recent informal advice letters the FPPC has issued
in In re: Sybert (No. I-91-297) and In re: Winston (No. I-90-230).

Moreover, I am interested in your opinion concerning an area not specifically
addressed in Sybert and Winston related to "foreseeability" of potential effects on indirect
economic interests. Using the Shell matter as an example, would the revenue generated
by Crosby's representation of Shell in the tort liability matters be taken into account in
assessing the "foreseeability" issue?

The Shell matter is scheduled to be heard by the Regional Board on October 21,
1992. Therefore, I would greatly appreciate your guidance as soon as possible. I
appreciate your attention to and assistance with this matter. Prior to issuing your formal
advice letter, I would appreciate the opportunity to speak with you if you have any
questions concerning the factual background or other elements of this request.

Very truly yours,

Craig S.J. Johns

CSJJ:bs
Enclosure

cc:    Gary Grimm, Esq.
       William Atwater, Esq.
       Steve Ritchie
